metrics.narrative: The buyer's pain is qualitative and well-articulated but not quantified in dollars or hours. On the 29 Apr 2026 scenario demo, an SFV attendee stated manual processes risk breaches and financial penalties if obligations are missed, and the team confirmed obligations are currently tracked manually via spreadsheets and email. No dollar value, contract-count, or penalty-exposure figure has been captured anywhere in the record.

champion.narrative: Luke Davies has been the consistent buyer-side relay of status throughout the deal (reporting delays, reengagement timing, and priority conflicts from Dec 2025 through Jul 2026), and a 16 Mar 2026 note relayed a stated buyer preference for Zycus. However, there is no evidence he is actively advocating internally, escalating on Zycus's behalf, or has access to the economic buyer, and the rep's own 12 Jun 2026 note records concern about the lack of engagement from the buyer side. His formal title/role at SFV is not confirmed in Salesforce contact roles.

competition.narrative: Ironclad is the only other named vendor and the RFP is explicitly a two-vendor race: the 25 Mar 2026 note records SFV's PM being instructed to issue the RFP to 'both shortlisted vendors.' On 13 Apr 2026 the buyer flagged a gap in Zycus's obligation-tracking capability but still wanted a response and demo, with Zycus committing to describe what it would add within 12 months, an acknowledged area where Ironclad may have stronger out-of-the-box capability.

sources: Avoma call notes, Zycus CLM Demo, 29 Apr 2026 (background, need, impact, current_workflow notes)
identify_pain.narrative: Pain is specific and buyer-confirmed: managing obligations on long-term, high-value energy contracts is a stated priority, current tracking is manual (spreadsheets and email), and an SFV attendee flagged the risk of breaches and financial penalties from missed obligations. The buyer also asked detailed questions about automated reminders, business-day-aware deadlines, and escalation workflows, showing the pain is actively felt by the operational team.

paper_process.narrative: Final commercials were submitted and a Letter of Intent was offered by the buyer on 16 Mar 2026, with the rep pushing hard for contract signing at that time. There is no evidence of any MSA, SOW, or further paper movement since, and the deal has instead cycled back into RFP evaluation and requirements review for the following three-plus months.

sources: OpportunityContactRole (Nick Middleton, Deputy General Counsel & Head of Contract Management)
economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer or budget approver has been identified across any source on this seven-plus-month, $110,000 opportunity. Nick Middleton (Deputy General Counsel & Head of Contract Management) is the most senior named contact role on record but shows no direct evidence of engagement in either read call. The 10 Feb 2026 Next Step note ('waiting on internal green light,' '1 higher priority project in motion') implies budget/priority decisions sit with someone above the working contacts, but that person is never named.

decision_process.narrative: The process is a two-vendor RFP: SFV's PM was instructed to issue the RFP to both shortlisted vendors on 25 Mar 2026, Zycus's response was submitted and a scenario demo delivered 29 Apr 2026, and feedback was due 01 May 2026. Since then the decision has been delayed four times (11 May, 25 May, 09 Jun, 12 Jun 2026) for reasons including additional ERP-integration requirements and an internal IT-led selection delay. No confirmed evaluation outcome or next decision date exists as of 09 Jul 2026.

decision_criteria.narrative: Evaluation criteria are well documented from the 29 Apr 2026 scenario-based RFP demo: three explicit use cases were scoped (end-to-end obligation tracking with automated alerts and a personal task dashboard, contract variation workflows with version history/approval logic/eSignature, and supplier performance data ingestion linked to obligations). Security and data-safety for the multi-tenant SaaS product were also raised as a criterion on the 02 Mar 2026 demo.

confidence_signals.summary: The deal is live (buyer replied 02 Jul 2026) but showing sustained cooling behavior: repeated short delays, an unconfirmed feedback loop, and no firm re-engagement date as of 06 Jul 2026.

north_star_verdict.math: Stage and close date unknown in Salesforce. Deal entered Shortlisted stage prior to 11 Mar 2026 per field history. Now 120+ days later with no buyer decision. LastActivityDate 03 Jul 2026 is rep-initiated outreach (awaiting buyer reply). No two-way buyer engagement since at least 13 May 2026 per call history. Four documented delays: ERP integration questions (11 May), requirements review (13 May), IT delay (24 Jun), decision delayed further (24 Jun). Buyer will reengage 'shortly' but no date. Time-in-stage exceeds enterprise norm (90-120d). No forward velocity. Stalled.

north_star_verdict.headline: Cold 116+ days in selection without decision; four successive buyer delays since March; EB unmapped; no buyer engagement in 6 days after rep-initiated outreach; unknown close date; obligation-tracking product gap vs Ironclad.

flags: Map the economic buyer and secure executive sponsorship — no budget-holder or CFO/CPO engaged; commercial sign-off path unknown; deal is secondary priority competing with higher-priority internal project (10 Feb Next Step), creating indefinite-stall and budget-reallocation risk.
Single-threaded: Luke Davies is the only active buyer contact; no Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, or champion engagement documented.
Best Case forecast is indefensible: no EB, passive champion, 116-day stall, and 55 days to compress remaining steps; recommend Omitted.

Multi-thread beyond Luke Davies: Nick Middleton, Shane Capps, Ahmad Salar and Chitra Shende have all attended a Zycus call but none correspond directly by email; open a second live channel so the deal doesn't run through one Operations Analyst relaying messages.

Multi-thread beyond Luke Davies to Nick Middleton (Deputy General Counsel, the likely DM) and the IT/finance stakeholders — single point of contact (Luke) is passive, engagement is cooling (12 Jun 'concerned about lack of engagement'), and no internal advocacy or EB access has been demonstrated.

Neutralize Ironclad's likely edge on obligation/task management by putting the 29 Apr 2026 roadmap commitment (dashboard + escalations within 12 months) and Merlin's Gen AI obligation-extraction capability in writing before the buyer's internal review concludes.
No two-way buyer engagement in 90+ days; deal COLD despite LastActivityDate 2026-07-03 (rep-side activity only)
best_practice_check.summary: The single highest-leverage move is mapping and engaging a real economic buyer on this stalled evaluation; secondary levers are closing the obligation-tracking gap versus Ironclad and broadening beyond the single, passive point of contact.

customer_preference.evidence: No buyer-voiced preference is confirmed within the last 30-60 days. The most recent preference statement (buyer preferred Zycus, LOI offered) dates to 16 Mar 2026, roughly 16 weeks ago, and is background context only under the recency rule, not a live signal.

competitive_position.summary: This is a two-vendor RFP between Zycus and Ironclad. Ironclad's threat is not confirmed as buyer-preferred, but Zycus carries an acknowledged capability gap on native obligation tracking (flagged 13 Apr 2026) that plays into whatever Ironclad's out-of-the-box strength is there. The buyer's current state (manual tracking via spreadsheets/email) is itself a passive competitor in the form of inertia.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A small ($110k), long-running APAC opportunity that is genuinely live (buyer replied 02 Jul 2026) but structurally weak: no economic buyer, a passive champion, an unresolved product gap versus Ironclad, and five successive close-date slips with no firm re-engagement date as of 06 Jul 2026.

deal_risk.text: 118 days in Shortlisted (since 13 Mar 2026) against a close date only 53 days out (31 Aug 2026) leaves little room for selection, commercials, and contracting at the current pace.
10 Feb 2026 note records this as a lower-priority project internally ('1 higher priority project in motion'), raising the risk of indefinite deferral or budget reallocation.

win_position.text: No economic buyer mapped after 7+ months — the most senior named contact, Nick Middleton (Deputy General Counsel & Head of Contract Management), has no evidenced direct engagement in either read call.
Buyer stated a clear preference for Zycus and offered an LOI on 16 Mar 2026, though this signal is now roughly four months stale with no reconfirmation.
Ironclad remains the only other vendor in the RFP, and Zycus's own team acknowledged an obligation-tracking capability gap on 13 Apr 2026 that plays to Ironclad's likely strength.
Champion Luke Davies is relaying delays but shows no evidence of internal advocacy; the rep's own 12 Jun 2026 note records concern about lack of buyer engagement.

deal_momentum.text: Four to five successive decision delays since Mar 2026 (11 May, 25 May, 09 Jun, 12 Jun 2026), most recently tied to an unresolved ERP-integration question.
The deal is not dark: the buyer sent a genuine reply on 02 Jul 2026 and Luke Davies indicated on 06 Jul 2026 that SFV will be ready to reengage shortlisted vendors shortly.
No firm re-engagement date has been given as of 06 Jul 2026; the rep himself rates confidence at only 50%.
